Stripe object shape.question: For 07-16, how should we map Stripe test Products and Prices?
options_presented: Selected smoke items only - create real Stripe test Products/Prices only for 1-2 canonical smoke variants, enough to prove the real integration without turning setup into catalog migration.
Every current store item - create test Products/Prices for all current visible store items, closer to production readiness but more setup and maintenance.
One generic test product - fastest Stripe proof, but weaker because it does not validate real BlackBox variant mappings.

Stripe object shape.question: Which selected variants should get real Stripe test mappings?
Stripe object shape.answer: One release item plus one distro item
options_presented: Canonical store smoke item only - disintegration-black-vinyl-lp; validates the known checkout path with the least setup.
One release + one distro item - canonical release smoke item plus one distro entry; proves both store-item sources.
Two highest-risk items - choose based on whichever items have the most distinct content/availability shape, even if not the canonical smoke route.

Stripe object shape.question: How should Stripe test object naming work?
options_presented: Mirror shopper-facing names - product names match the public item option names, with metadata carrying app IDs like storeItemSlug and variantId.
Prefix all test objects - names start with BlackBox Test - ...; clearer in Stripe, slightly less like production.
Operational SKU style - compact names/codes optimized for internal lookup, less readable in Stripe Dashboard.

Stripe object shape.question: Should the price_* values live only in ignored local seed SQL, or should we also define a non-secret mapping procedure for sandbox D1?
Stripe object shape.answer: Local ignored seed plus sandbox manual command
options_presented: Local ignored seed + sandbox manual command - keep actual price_* values out of git, document the exact local and sandbox D1 insert/update commands with placeholders.
Local ignored seed only - enough for local validation, but leaves hosted sandbox setup less repeatable.
Document Stripe Dashboard only - no repo-side mapping procedure beyond saying where to add the IDs.
